classification_policy.canonical_fact: A recurring story or production fact stable across competing major versions; wording and implementation may remain unresolved.
classification_policy.approved_reference: An artifact with explicit user approval or an authoritative selected-version record.
classification_policy.acceptable_alternative: A reviewed alternative that preserves approved semantics within declared tolerances.
classification_policy.experiment: An attempt or branch with evidence of creation but no evidence of selection.
classification_policy.superseded: An artifact observably replaced within a declared lineage; local supersession does not imply project-wide rejection.
classification_policy.unresolved: Evidence whose authority, mapping, or conflict cannot be decided from the snapshot.
classification_policy.primary_classification_rule: Each artifact receives exactly one primary classification per group. Lineage relationships are recorded separately and do not create a second classification.

canonical_facts: A guardian defends a fantasy frontier from a mechanical threat.
A scout, two drum-associated attendants, and a tiger companion participate in the fantasy layer.
Battle or charge action transitions into a warm domestic reality.
Fantasy characters and locations map to household objects and toys.
Damage to the guardian is repaired and reinterpreted as honorable protection.
project_level.blocking_reason: No approval flag, selected-version record, final-master identifier, or complete edit decision list exists in the snapshot.

canonical_facts: The story opens with failed defenses and an approaching mechanical threat.
The threat handoff leads into the guardian's point of view.
